title: Never commit, tag, push, or publish autonomously
body: All git commit, git tag, git push, and luarocks upload actions must be explicitly triggered by the user. The agent never runs them on its own initiative, even when work is complete and verified.

title: Never edit workspace files via terminal commands
body: Do not use sed -i, echo >, tee, or similar shell redirection to modify tracked workspace files. Always use the dedicated file-editing tools (Edit/Write) so changes are reviewable.

title: copilot-instructions.md and copilot-planer.md are local-only, never pushed
body: The two files under .github/ (copilot-instructions.md, copilot-planer.md) are gitignored and must never be committed or pushed. copilot-instructions holds architectural decisions; copilot-planer holds phased plans.

title: eval/results is gitignored except longmemeval.md
body: Everything under eval/results/ is local-only except longmemeval.md, which is a force-added tracked exception. locomo.md, convomem.md, and all *.json result files are never committed unless git add -f is used deliberately.

title: All Lua code must be Lua 5.1 compatible
body: luamemo runs on lua5.1 (PUC). No goto, no bitwise operators, no bit32. This constraint bit the indexer build three times. Anything needing bit ops must use pure arithmetic instead.

title: Postgres access uses pgmoon, not luadbi
body: The DB layer is built on pgmoon (the OpenResty/Leafo Postgres client), chosen over luadbi. pgmoon connects automatically from PGHOST/PGDATABASE/PGUSER/PGPASSWORD env vars and is LuaJIT-friendly.

title: The hash embedder is lexical feature-hashing, not semantic
body: luamemo's hash embedder is signed feature hashing over word tokens plus character trigrams (the Weinberger hashing trick). It has no learned semantics and cannot capture meaning; car and automobile hash to unrelated dimensions. Its role is a zero-dependency default, overlapping Postgres FTS.

title: Hybrid search candidate pool is vector-nearest UNION top-FTS
body: store.search's pgvector path builds its candidate pool as the union of the vector-nearest rows and the top FTS matches, instead of vector-nearest only. This lets FTS-only and lexically-distant rows enter the pool. COALESCE guards keep NULL-embedding rows from corrupting rank order.

title: no_embed inserts a NULL embedding for FTS-only rows
body: store.write_many accepts a per-row no_embed=true flag that inserts a NULL embedding and skips the embed call. Such rows still surface via the FTS leg. The codebase index stores file rows this way by default.

title: Two backends: bruteforce (real[]) and pgvector (vector(N)+HNSW)
body: The bruteforce backend stores embeddings as Postgres real[] (dimension-agnostic, exact cosine in Lua). The pgvector backend uses a fixed vector(N) column with an HNSW index (approximate). backend=auto detects which from the schema.

title: The MCP server self-locates its modules from arg[0]
body: mcp/server.lua derives the repo/plugin root from arg[0] at startup and prepends it to package.path, so require works without LUA_PATH and a stale LuaRocks install cannot shadow the bundled modules.

title: SessionStart hook must emit hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext
body: To inject context at session start, the hook must output the hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext JSON form. Plain stdout is NOT reliably injected into the session.

title: Codebase-index file rows are FTS-only by default
body: During index ingest, file rows get no_embed=true (no vector) unless --embed-file-rows is set. Their body is enriched with split path words plus a 'defines: <symbol names>' digest so lexical/path queries match. Symbols, deps, and diffs still embed.

title: universal-ctags is optional enrichment for non-native languages
body: For languages without a native pure-Lua parser (go, rust, ruby, java, c), luamemo/index/ctags.lua shells out to universal-ctags with JSON output to extract symbols. Native parsers win for lua/py/js/ts because they also give docstrings and imports. Disabled by MEMO_INDEX_NO_CTAGS=1.

title: Checksums use DJB2, not FNV-1a
body: The index change-detection checksum uses DJB2 (pure arithmetic) instead of FNV-1a, because FNV requires a bitwise XOR that Lua 5.1 lacks without the bit library. DJB2 is equally suitable for change detection.

title: The map locates; disk is ground truth
body: The codebase index is a map that tells the agent where relevant code is. It is not the source of truth: before editing, the agent reads the actual file from disk. Use index_search to find, then read the file.

title: Scope naming conventions
body: Memory scopes: global (facts true everywhere), repo:<name> (project-specific), session:<uuid> (ephemeral), diary:<agent> (agent reflections), codeindex:<project> (the codebase map).

title: pgvector HNSW matches bruteforce exact search to <=0.4pp
body: A 2026-07 sweep showed the pgvector HNSW backend matches the exact bruteforce backend to within 0.4pp R@1 and 0.002 MRR at both 768-dim (nomic) and 1024-dim (bge-m3). Approximate search costs essentially no recall here.

title: Cross-encoder reranking is a no-op on the prose benchmarks
body: bge-reranker-v2-m3 moved recall ~0 at both rerank_top_n=20 and 100. Retrieved golds are already optimally ordered by bge-m3, and missed golds sit outside even a 100-candidate pool. Levers that act on ordering cannot fix gaps that are about coverage.

title: TEI hangs (no error) on inputs over MAX_BATCH_TOKENS
body: The TEI bge-m3 sidecar stalls the full request timeout instead of erroring when a single input exceeds MAX_BATCH_TOKENS. ~30% of LongMemEval sessions exceed the old 4096 default. Fix: raise to 7168 (the 6GB RTX 2060 VRAM ceiling; 8192 OOMs), set AUTO_TRUNCATE=true, cap bodies via EMBED_MAX_CHARS.

title: Embedder quality ranking on these benchmarks: bge-m3 > nomic > hash
body: bge-m3 (1024-dim) beats ollama nomic-embed-text (768-dim) by ~5.4pp R@1 on LoCoMo and saturates LME oracle at 100%; hash is far below both (54% on LME). The embedder is the single biggest recall lever.

title: Target EmbeddingGemma (the embedder), not a generative Gemma
body: For the in-process FFI embedder, the right open-weights artifact is EmbeddingGemma (~308M params, Matryoshka dims 768/512/256/128, on-device oriented) — an embedding model. A generative Gemma is the wrong tool for embeddings.

title: Learn from corrections and outcomes, not access frequency
body: The learned-from-usage layer weights corrections (user/agent overrides) and outcomes (memory led to a good result) above raw access frequency. Frequency approximately equals importance is a trap that learns the FAQ and cements bad early decisions.

title: The retrieval benchmarks are a regression rail, not the success metric
body: LME/LoCoMo/ConvoMem measure retrieval recall only. They prove a learned or owned layer does not break generic retrieval; they cannot see personalization or durability. Success is measured by the project-personalization eval.

title: Ordering levers can't fix coverage gaps
body: Three sweeps (hybrid-union, pgvector-vs-bruteforce, reranking) all scored ~0 on recall because the gaps on these datasets are about coverage — getting the gold into the pool — not ordering within it. Coverage levers are better embeddings, chunking, and query-side work.

title: Eval Postgres databases
body: Benchmark DBs: lm_pgv_tei (vector 1024, bge-m3), lm_pgv_ollama (vector 768, nomic), lm_bruteforce_test (real[], serves any embedder via scope isolation). Build a pgvector DB by sed-replacing vector(384) in schema.sql to the embedder dim, then applying migrations 002-009.

title: Tests and dev default to the hash embedder
body: MEMO_EMBEDDER=hash is the default for tests and local dev — fast, pure-Lua, no service. The dev database is postgres://postgres:postgres@127.0.0.1:5432/luamemo_dev. Ollama nomic and a TEI bge-m3 sidecar are also available for benchmarks.

title: Owning the embedder is the durability play
body: Memory survives LLM-provider swaps for free (it lives in Postgres keyed by the embedder, not the LLM). The only coupling is the embedder — changing it forces a re-embed. Owning the embedder (pinned EmbeddingGemma via FFI, or the pure-math hash+projection path) makes memory vendor-proof.

title: The learned reranker is judged by the personalization eval, not recall
body: A learned reranker reorders candidates, so it scores ~0 on the frozen retrieval benchmarks by construction. Its value is personalization; it is gated by the frozen suite as a no-regression rail AND by the project-personalization eval as the actual success metric.

title: write_many batches embeds and inserts
body: store.write_many embeds queued rows concurrently via luamemo.async and compresses inserts into multi-VALUES statements per chunk. Rows with no_embed=true bypass embed and dedup. The async batch shares one deadline of embed_timeout_ms times the queue size.
